query: Japan Imperial House Law bill submission Diet May 2026 皇室典範 国会 法律案 提出
answer:
## Research Report: Japan Imperial House Law (皇室典範) Bill Submission to the Diet — May 2026

### Executive Summary

**As of May 22, 2026, no formal Imperial House Law (皇室典範) amendment bill has been submitted to the Japanese Diet.** However, the legislative process is at an advanced pre-submission stage, with the Speakers of both Houses actively drafting a "consensus of the legislature" (立法府の総意) proposal expected to be presented imminently — possibly within days. The ruling LDP has pledged to achieve passage of an Imperial House Law amendment during the current Diet session.

---

### Current Status (May 22, 2026)

- **No bill is listed** in the official 217th Diet session bill registries (checked on both Shugiin.go.jp and Sangiin.go.jp). Zero matches for 皇室典範 among cabinet-submitted bills (閣法), House of Representatives member bills (衆法), or House of Councillors member bills (参法).
- The current Diet session is a **special session (特別国会)** following the February 2026 House of Representatives election.
- The legislative process is at the **inter-party consensus-building stage**, with a formal "consensus of the legislature" draft expected imminently.

---

### Key Timeline (2026)

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| Feb 18 | House Speaker Mori Eisuke declares imperial succession reform "an urgent matter that cannot be delayed" |
| Mar 16 | PM Takaichi says she values advisory panel proposals; LDP and JIP agree to prioritize adoption of former male imperial family members |
| Mar 18 | PM Takaichi approves female members keeping status after marriage but rejects female emperor |
| Apr 14 | PM Takaichi declares "Revision to the Imperial House Law is urgently needed" |
| Apr 15 | First plenary meeting of current session; Speaker Mori states goal: "Imperial House Law amendment — passage this Diet session" (皇室典範改正、今国会成立を) |
| Apr 27 | LDP conservative lawmakers submit proposal (not a bill) to the government opposing women keeping titles after marriage |
| May 7 | Chudo Kaikaku Rengo (CRA) finalizes stance: conditionally backs adoption of former imperial males |
| May 12 | All 13 parties/groups finalize their positions |
| **May 15** | **Full inter-party plenary meeting at Speaker's residence; all 13 parties present views; Speaker Mori announces Speakers will draft "consensus of the legislature" proposal "as early as next week"** |
| May 18 | LDP Vice President Aso Taro and Policy Research Council Chair Kobayashi meet; Kobayashi pledges "reliable amendment of the Imperial House Law this session" (今国会での皇室典範の確実な改正) |
| May 20-22 | Asahi reports legislative consensus proposal expected "soon" (近く示される) |

---

### The Two Core Proposals Under Consideration

1. **Female imperial members retaining status after marriage** — Broadly supported (65% public approval in May 17 Asahi poll; majority of parties favor). Debate remains over whether husbands/children would gain imperial status.

2. **Adoption of male-line males from former imperial branch families (旧宮家復帰案)** — More politically contested. The LDP treats this as "first priority" (reflecting conservative wing influence, particularly Aso Taro). Most opposition parties conditionally accept but do not prioritize it.

---

### Public Opinion (May 2026)

- **Asahi poll (May 16-17):** 65% support female members remaining after marriage; 19% opposed; >70% support both female emperor and female-line succession
- **Shukan Bunshun poll (May 20):** 93% of 25,000 respondents favor a female emperor; 88.9% favor matrilineal succession
- **Massive gap** between public opinion and LDP's male-line-only stance

---

### Political Dynamics

- **LDP/ruling coalition:** Prioritizes male-line adoption; opposes female emperor; PM Takaichi explicitly states male succession line to Prince Hisahito "must not be disrupted"
- **CDP (main opposition):** Favors female succession; does not support adoption proposal
- **CRA (centrist):** Conditionally backs adoption; prioritizes female members remaining
- **Public opinion:** Overwhelmingly favors female succession (70-93%)

---

### What Comes Next

The "consensus of the legislature" draft was expected to be presented during the week of May 19-23. Once this consensus is reached, a formal amendment bill would be drafted and submitted. The LDP has expressed intent to achieve passage this session, though at least one party representative was quoted saying passage within the session would be "impossible" (無理だろう).

---

### Sources

| Source | Date | URL |
|--------|------|-----|
| Nikkei Shimbun | Apr 15, 2026 | https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXZQOUA1403W0U6A410C2000000/ |
| Asahi Shimbun | May 15, 2026 | https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASV5H2S8CV5HUTIL00KM.html |
| Asahi Shimbun | May 15, 2026 | https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASV5H3DZNV5HUTFK00FM.html |
| Nikkei Shimbun | May 18, 2026 | https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXZQOUA187WW0Y6A510C2000000/ |
| Asahi Shimbun | May 17, 2026 | https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASV5K2QV8V5KUZPS002M.html |
| Asahi Shimbun | May 22, 2026 | https://www.asahi.com/articles/DA3S16467647.html |
| Nikkei Shimbun | May 12, 2026 | https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXZQOUA123ZU0S6A510C2000000/ |
| Nippon.com | Mar 16, 2026 | nippon.com |
| Nippon.com | May 7, 2026 | nippon.com |

Asahi poll (May 16-17): 65% support female members remaining; >70% support female emperor and female-line succession
